title: Flaky test on s390x: TestCheckLUKSPassphrase/v2
body:
While preparing the next upload of buildah 1.37.2 to Debian, I noticed build failures on s390x (and that arch only).

All build failures  showed unusual behavior, (possibly indicating memory corruption?) in the test TestCheckLUKSPassphrase/v2 since version 1.37.0

- https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.0%2Bds1-1&stamp=1723481252&raw=0
- https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.1%2Bds1-1&stamp=1723773224&raw=0
- https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.1%2Bds1-2&stamp=1723904120&raw=0
- https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.2%2Bds1-1&stamp=1725229001&raw=0
- https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.2%2Bds1-1&stamp=1725234835&raw=0

Test output:

```
[...]
=== RUN   TestCheckLUKSPassphrase/v2/sectorSize=512
runtime: marked free object in span 0x3ff9bf41c28, elemsize=8 freeindex=177 (bad use of unsafe.Pointer? try -d=checkptr)
0xc000112000 alloc marked  
0xc000112008 alloc marked  
0xc000112010 alloc marked  
[...]
0xc000113f78 free  unmarked
fatal error: found pointer to free object

	/<<PKGBUILDDIR>>/_build/src/golang.org/x/crypto/argon2/argon2.go:232 +0x184
FAIL	github.com/containers/buildah/internal/mkcw	7.925s
```

comments.body:
I wonder whether https://github.com/containers/luksy/commit/dc16bfe82b90dff5fa6da9466aa598e41791e375 would be related to this? the logs above depict builds with a copy of luksy that lack that commit. 

I've just updated luksy to the most recent version in Debian and will update buildah to build against a copy of luksy with that patch.
nope, no difference, still seeing crash: https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=golang-github-containers-buildah&arch=s390x&ver=1.37.3%2Bds1-1&stamp=1727347791&raw=0

Was able to reproduce this quite easily on a s390x machine using a checkout of buildah (I used tag v1.37.7):

`go test -race -gcflags="-d=checkptr" github.com/containers/buildah/internal/mkcw`

I've pasted the output at https://gist.github.com/siretart/b6726406066ba1d80088edd19a133304
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
